answer:
\boxed{B}  

The study found that infusion of all solutions, including those with low-concentration epinephrine, had no effect on any measured hemodynamic variable, including uterine blood flow. Only the intravenous bolus of epinephrine (15 micrograms) significantly decreased uterine blood flow. Therefore, continuous infusion of low-concentration epinephrine did not impair uterine blood flow.
